added.description: In a tribute to and send-up of the Pulitzer Prize-winning play, "August: Osage County," a dark secret is revealed about the Smith family's past when Francine invites Stan's incarcerated dad, Jack, to Stan's birthday party. Also in this spoof, Roger plays Edna, a maid who nurses Bullock and Hayley's love child. But when Edna falls in love with Bullock, it could mean dire consequences for the kid.
added.title: Blood Crieth Unto Heaven

added.description: The Smith family forgets to celebrate Father's Day, and so to make it up to Stan, they plan out the perfect day. Stan uses a CIA device to erase their memories and have them continually plan perfect days for him.
added.title: Father's Daze

added.description: Stan goes over-the-top to produce a reenactment of Charles Lindbergh's solo flight for Steve's school project after he gets an F. Klaus buys a pair of shoes online and Hayley helps him harass the neighbor they believe has stolen them.
added.title: Fight and Flight

added.description: Stan uses a mothballed military computer to do Steve's math homework, starting the clock toward nuclear annihilation.
added.title: Guardian

added.description: After Greg receives a plaque from the local historical society, Stan goes to great lengths to get the same honor. Roger becomes a traveling salesman.
added.title: Oh Brothel, Where Art Thou?

added.description: To save their marriage, Jeff needs Hayley to open up more to him, but she wants no part in it.
added.title: Pork 'N Feelings

added.description: Stan is embarrassed when a painting of Francine's genitals, done by a famous artist, is unveiled at the museum. Steve turns to helping people after masturbation is ruined by his mom's portrait.
added.title: Portrait of Francine's Genitals

added.description: Steve spends his summer working on an app with some unlikely friends, while the rest of the family become cord-cutters.
added.title: Silicon Steve

added.description: Hayley takes Roger with her to a meditation center and he quickly takes over, becoming one of the world's leading authorities on meditation. Stan and Steve become apprentices as they are taught the art of making sushi.
added.title: The Enlightenment of Ragi-Baba

added.description: When Rogu comes down with a mysterious illness, the Smiths struggle to find a cure.
added.title: The Sickness

added.description: An aspiring inventor leaves the Farm to make a go of it in The Big City.
added.title: What Great Advancements!

added.description: Louise suspects there's something sinister afoot during a Halloween field trip to an apple orchard.
added.title: Apple Gore-Chard! (But Not Gory)

added.description: The trio have a falling out and go their separate ways, while a typhoon ominously approaches.
added.title: The Motley Flock Scatters

added.description: Space Ghost presents a special tribute to Zorak. Dr. Maxcy Nolan, professor of entomology at the University of Georgia, speculates that the bandleader may be hiding an extra set of legs under his vest. Exterminator Steve Arnold, meanwhile, reveals that he says "Take this!" before going after insects.
added.title: Zorak
tags: sgc2c space ghost coast to coast episode 41 zorak maxcy nolan legs steve arnold insects

removed.description: Bob competes in a Bachelor-style competition to inherit Mr. Fischoeder's 100-year-old giant tortoise.
removed.title: The Shell Game

removed.description: After Hayley is deemed a "big child" by The Lady from The Hamster Rescue Society, she gets involved with two Roger personas.
removed.title: An Adult Woman

removed.description: After a nearly fatal interaction with one of Roger's personas, Steve and Stan work together to uncover the persona's mysterious past.
removed.title: Piece by Piece

removed.description: Bullock throws a private, star-studded Christmas party the exact same night as the CIA work party. Stan and Klaus are determined to get in.
removed.title: Nasty Christmas

removed.description: Jeff gets a job after the family calls him a mooch. When he unexpectedly strikes it rich, Hayley must protect Jeff from the family's greed.
removed.title: Idiot Rich

removed.description: Hayley scores an internship at Morning Mimosa but finds herself on thin ice when she tries to inject real journalism into the show.
removed.title: Killer Mimosa
